transport.c(3) Library Functions Manual transport.c(3)

NAME

transport.c - WebSocket transport implementation for ACIP protocol.

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING 64
Maximum incoming message queue size (frames received from peer). #define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING 512
Maximum outgoing message queue size (frames to send to peer). #define WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING
#define WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING

Functions


acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char *name, const char *url, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket client transport. acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create (const char *name, struct lws *wsi, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection.

Detailed Description

WebSocket transport implementation for ACIP protocol.

Implements the acip_transport_t interface for WebSocket connections. Enables browser clients to connect via WebSocket protocol.

ARCHITECTURE:

  • Uses libwebsockets for WebSocket protocol handling
  • Async libwebsockets callbacks bridge to sync recv() via ringbuffer
  • Thread-safe receive queue handles async message arrival
  • Same pattern as WebRTC transport for consistency

MESSAGE FLOW:

1.
send(): Synchronous write via lws_write()
2.
LWS callback: Async write to receive ringbuffer
3.
recv(): Blocking read from receive ringbuffer

MEMORY OWNERSHIP:

  • Transport OWNS wsi (WebSocket instance)
  • Receive queue owns buffered message data
  • recv() allocates message buffer, caller must free

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file websocket/transport.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING 64

Maximum incoming message queue size (frames received from peer). Set to 64 for balance: allows buffering across one large frame's fragments while keeping latency reasonable. With ~70 fragments per 291KB frame, we need at least 64 slots to avoid dropping the frame while it's fragmenting.

Definition at line 56 of file websocket/transport.c.

#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING 512

Maximum outgoing message queue size (frames to send to peer). Set to 64 to match incoming queue and prevent "BINARY after FIN" errors. Large video frames fragment into ~70 chunks; queue must be large enough to buffer one complete message worth of fragments.

Definition at line 65 of file websocket/transport.c.

#define WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING

Definition at line 68 of file websocket/transport.c.

Referenced by acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), and acip_websocket_server_transport_create().

#define WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING

Definition at line 69 of file websocket/transport.c.

Referenced by acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), and acip_websocket_server_transport_create().

Function Documentation

acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char * name, const char * url, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)

Create WebSocket client transport.

Parameters

url WebSocket URL (e.g., "ws://localhost:27225")
crypto_ctx Optional encryption context (can be NULL)

Returns

Transport instance or NULL on failure

Definition at line 1289 of file websocket/transport.c.

1290                                                                                        {
1291   if (!name) {
1292     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1293     return NULL;
1294   }
1295 
1296   if (!url) {
1297     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "url is required");
1298     return NULL;
1299   }
1300 
1301   // Parse URL to extract host, port, and path
1302   // Format: ws://host:port/path or wss://host:port/path
1303   const char *protocol_end = strstr(url, "://");
1304   if (!protocol_end) {
1305     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid WebSocket URL format (missing ://)");
1306     return NULL;
1307   }
1308 
1309   bool use_ssl = (strncmp(url, "wss://", 6) == 0);
1310   const char *host_start = protocol_end + 3;
1311 
1312   // Find port (if specified)
1313   const char *port_start = strchr(host_start, ':');
1314   const char *path_start = strchr(host_start, '/');
1315 
1316   char host[256] = {0};
1317   int port = use_ssl ? 443 : 27226; // Default: wss:// uses 443, ws:// uses 27226 (ascii-chat WebSocket port)
1318   char path[256] = "/";
1319 
1320   if (port_start && (!path_start || port_start < path_start)) {
1321     // Port is specified
1322     size_t host_len = port_start - host_start;
1323     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1324       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1325       return NULL;
1326     }
1327     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1328     host[host_len] = '\0';
1329 
1330     // Extract port
1331     port = atoi(port_start + 1);
1332     if (port <= 0 || port > 65535) {
1333       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid port number");
1334       return NULL;
1335     }
1336   } else {
1337     // No port specified, use default
1338     size_t host_len = path_start ? (size_t)(path_start - host_start) : strlen(host_start);
1339     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1340       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1341       return NULL;
1342     }
1343     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1344     host[host_len] = '\0';
1345   }
1346 
1347   // Extract path
1348   if (path_start) {
1349     SAFE_STRNCPY(path, path_start, sizeof(path) - 1);
1350     path[sizeof(path) - 1] = '\0';
1351   }
1352 
1353   log_info("Connecting to WebSocket: %s (host=%s, port=%d, path=%s, ssl=%d)", url, host, port, path, use_ssl);
1354 
1355   // Allocate transport structure
1356   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1357   if (!transport) {
1358     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1359     return NULL;
1360   }
1361 
1362   // Allocate WebSocket-specific data
1363   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1364       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1365   if (!ws_data) {
1366     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1367     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1368     return NULL;
1369   }
1370 
1371   // Initialize destruction state flag (CALLOC zero-initializes, but explicit for clarity)
1372   atomic_store_bool(&ws_data->is_destroying, false);
1373 
1374   // Create receive queue
1375   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1376   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1377     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1378     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1379     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1380     return NULL;
1381   }
1382 
1383   // Create send queue for client transport to buffer outgoing messages
1384   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1385   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1386     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1387     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1388     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1389     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1390     return NULL;
1391   }
1392 
1393   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1394   char recv_name[64], state_name[64];
1395   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1396   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1397 
1398   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1399     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1400     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1401     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1402     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1403     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1404     return NULL;
1405   }
1406 
1407   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1408     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1409     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1410     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1411     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1412     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1413     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1414     return NULL;
1415   }
1416 
1417   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1418     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1419     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1420     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1421     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1422     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1423     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1424     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1425     return NULL;
1426   }
1427 
1428   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1429     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1430     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1431     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1432     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1433     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1434     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1435     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1436     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1437     return NULL;
1438   }
1439 
1440   // Allocate initial send buffer
1441   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = LWS_PRE + 524288; // Match tx_packet_size (512KB)
1442   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(ws_data->send_buffer_capacity, uint8_t *);
1443   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1444     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1445     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1446     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1447     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1448     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1449     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1450     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1451     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1452     return NULL;
1453   }
1454 
1455   // Create libwebsockets context
1456   // Protocol array must persist for lifetime of context - use static
1457   // IMPORTANT: "http" protocol MUST be first for WebSocket upgrade handshake to work
1458   static struct lws_protocols client_protocols[] = {
1459       {
1460           "http", // Required first for WebSocket upgrade
1461           websocket_callback,
1462           0,      // Per-session data (unused, using connect_info.userdata instead)
1463           524288, // RX buffer size
1464           0,      // ID
1465           NULL,   // User pointer (will be set from connect_info.userdata)
1466           524288  // TX packet size
1467       },
1468       {
1469           "acip", // ACIP protocol
1470           websocket_callback,
1471           0,      // Per-session data (unused, using connect_info.userdata instead)
1472           524288, // RX buffer size
1473           0,      // ID
1474           NULL,   // User pointer (will be set from connect_info.userdata)
1475           524288  // TX packet size
1476       },
1477       {NULL, NULL, 0, 0, 0, NULL, 0} // Terminator
1478   };
1479 
1480   // Disable client compression for now - causes assertion in lws_set_extension_option()
1481   // This is a known issue with libwebsockets permessage-deflate negotiation
1482   // Server-side compression is still enabled, data will be compressed from server to client
1483   // but client->server traffic remains uncompressed (acceptable since client sends less data)
1484   // static const struct lws_extension client_extensions[] = {
1485   //     {"permessage-deflate", lws_extension_callback_pm_deflate, "permessage-deflate; client_max_window_bits=15"},
1486   //     {NULL, NULL, NULL}};
1487 
1488   struct lws_context_creation_info info;
1489   memset(&info, 0, sizeof(info));
1490   info.port = CONTEXT_PORT_NO_LISTEN; // Client mode - no listening
1491   info.protocols = client_protocols;
1492   info.gid = (gid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1493   info.uid = (uid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1494   info.options = LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT;
1495   info.extensions = NULL; // Disable client compression due to lws_set_extension_option() assertion
1496 
1497   // Increase per-thread service buffer to prevent fragmentation of large messages
1498   // Default is 4KB, causing 291KB frames to fragment into 73 × 4KB chunks
1499   // Increase to 512KB to match server and allow larger WebSocket frames without fragmentation
1500   info.pt_serv_buf_size = 512 * 1024; // 512KB per-thread service buffer
1501 
1502   // Configure keep-alive to prevent idle disconnects during handshake
1503   // PING every 30 seconds if idle, close after 35 seconds total with no response
1504   static const lws_retry_bo_t client_keep_alive_policy = {
1505       .secs_since_valid_ping = 30,   // Send PING after 30s idle
1506       .secs_since_valid_hangup = 35, // Hangup if still idle after 35s
1507   };
1508   info.retry_and_idle_policy = &client_keep_alive_policy;
1509 
1510   // Enable libwebsockets logging through centralized logging system
1511   lws_log_init_client();
1512 
1513   ws_data->context = lws_create_context(&info);
1514 
1515   if (!ws_data->context) {
1516     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1517     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1518     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1519     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1520     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1521     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1522     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1523     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1524     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1525     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create libwebsockets context");
1526     return NULL;
1527   }
1528 
1529   // Connect to WebSocket server
1530   log_debug("Initiating WebSocket connection to %s:%d%s", host, port, path);
1531   struct lws_client_connect_info connect_info;
1532   memset(&connect_info, 0, sizeof(connect_info));
1533   connect_info.context = ws_data->context;
1534   connect_info.address = host;
1535   connect_info.port = port;
1536   connect_info.path = path;
1537   connect_info.host = host;
1538   connect_info.origin = host;
1539   connect_info.protocol = "acip";
1540   // Use SSL + skip server certificate hostname verification (for self-signed certs in development)
1541   connect_info.ssl_connection = use_ssl ? (LCCSCF_USE_SSL | LCCSCF_SKIP_SERVER_CERT_HOSTNAME_CHECK) : 0;
1542   connect_info.userdata = ws_data;
1543 
1544   log_debug("Calling lws_client_connect_via_info...");
1545   ws_data->wsi = lws_client_connect_via_info(&connect_info);
1546   log_debug("lws_client_connect_via_info returned: %p", (void *)ws_data->wsi);
1547   if (!ws_data->wsi) {
1548     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1549     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1550     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1551     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1552     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1553     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1554     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1555     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1556     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1557     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1558     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to connect to WebSocket server");
1559     return NULL;
1560   }
1561 
1562   ws_data->is_connected = false;      // Will be set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_ESTABLISHED
1563   ws_data->connection_failed = false; // Set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_CONNECTION_ERROR
1564   ws_data->owns_context = true;       // Client transport owns the context
1565 
1566   // Initialize transport
1567   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1568   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1569   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1570 
1571   // CRITICAL: Give libwebsockets time to initialize connection before starting service thread
1572   // libwebsockets needs to process the initial connection handshake callbacks before lws_service()
1573   // is called repeatedly. Concurrent access to context during connection setup can trigger assertions.
1574   // Sleep 50ms to let lws_client_connect_via_info() callbacks complete initialization.
1575   log_debug("Delaying service thread start to allow libwebsockets connection initialization...");
1576   platform_sleep_us(50000); // 50ms delay
1577 
1578   // Start service thread after connection initialization
1579   // Only the service thread should call lws_service() on this context
1580   log_debug("Starting WebSocket service thread...");
1581   ws_data->service_running = true;
1582   if (asciichat_thread_create(&ws_data->service_thread, "ws_service", websocket_service_thread, ws_data) != 0) {
1583     log_error("Failed to create WebSocket service thread");
1584     ws_data->service_running = false;
1585     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1586     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1587     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1588     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1589     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1590     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1591     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1592     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1593     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1594     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1595     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to create service thread");
1596     return NULL;
1597   }
1598   log_debug("WebSocket service thread started");
1599 
1600   // CRITICAL FIX: Do NOT block the main thread waiting for connection!
1601   // This prevents stdin/keyboard input from being processed and causes the client to hang.
1602   // The service thread will establish the connection asynchronously.
1603   // The protocol layer (recv) will detect if connection fails and handle it there.
1604   // Return immediately so the main thread can respond to keyboard input.
1605   log_debug("WebSocket transport created, service thread will establish connection asynchronously");
1606 
1607   // Register transport
1608   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1609 
1610   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1611   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1612 
1613   // Return transport immediately - connection will be established by service thread
1614   // If connection fails, recv() will detect is_connected=false and return error
1615   return transport;
1616 }




References asciichat_thread_create(),
    atomic_store_bool(), lws_log_init_client(),
    mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(), platform_sleep_us(),
    ringbuffer_create(), ringbuffer_destroy(),
    WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, and WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE.


Referenced by server_connection_establish(), and
    websocket_client_connect().






acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create
  (const char * name, struct lws * wsi, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)


Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection. Wraps
    an already-established libwebsockets connection (from server accept). Used
    by websocket_server module to create transports for incoming clients.




Parameters


wsi Established libwebsockets connection (not
  owned by transport)


crypto_ctx Optional crypto context


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on error


Definition at line 1628 of file
    websocket/transport.c.


1629                                                                                        {
1630   if (!name) {
1631     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1632     return NULL;
1633   }
1634 
1635   if (!wsi) {
1636     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid wsi parameter");
1637     return NULL;
1638   }
1639 
1640   // Allocate transport structure
1641   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1642   if (!transport) {
1643     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1644     return NULL;
1645   }
1646 
1647   // Allocate transport-specific data
1648   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1649       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1650   if (!ws_data) {
1651     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1652     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1653     return NULL;
1654   }
1655 
1656   // Initialize receive queue
1657   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1658   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1659     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1660     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1661     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1662     return NULL;
1663   }
1664 
1665   // Initialize send queue (for server-side transports)
1666   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1667   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1668     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1669     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1670     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1671     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1672     return NULL;
1673   }
1674 
1675   // Initialize pending-free queue for deferred buffer freeing
1676   // permessage-deflate compression holds buffer references asynchronously,
1677   // so we defer freeing to prevent use-after-free errors
1678   ws_data->pending_free_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(pending_free_item_t), 256);
1679   if (!ws_data->pending_free_queue) {
1680     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1681     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1682     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1683     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1684     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create pending-free queue");
1685     return NULL;
1686   }
1687 
1688   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1689   char recv_name[64], send_name[64], state_name[64], pending_free_name[64];
1690   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1691   snprintf(send_name, sizeof(send_name), "send_%s", name);
1692   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1693   snprintf(pending_free_name, sizeof(pending_free_name), "pending_free_%s", name);
1694 
1695   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1696     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1697     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1698     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1699     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1700     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1701     return NULL;
1702   }
1703 
1704   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1705     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1706     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1707     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1708     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1709     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1710     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1711     return NULL;
1712   }
1713 
1714   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->send_mutex, send_name) != 0) {
1715     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1716     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1717     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1718     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1719     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1720     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1721     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize send mutex");
1722     return NULL;
1723   }
1724 
1725   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1726     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1727     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1728     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1729     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1730     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1731     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1732     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1733     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1734     return NULL;
1735   }
1736 
1737   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1738     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1739     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1740     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1741     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1742     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1743     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1744     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1745     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1746     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1747     return NULL;
1748   }
1749 
1750   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->pending_free_mutex, pending_free_name) != 0) {
1751     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1752     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1753     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1754     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1755     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1756     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1757     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1758     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->pending_free_queue);
1759     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1760     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1761     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize pending-free mutex");
1762     return NULL;
1763   }
1764 
1765   // Allocate send buffer with LWS_PRE padding
1766   size_t initial_capacity = 4096 + LWS_PRE;
1767   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(initial_capacity, uint8_t *);
1768   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1769     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1770     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1771     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1772     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1773     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1774     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1775     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1776     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1777     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1778     return NULL;
1779   }
1780   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = initial_capacity;
1781 
1782   // Store connection info (server-side: no context ownership, connection already established)
1783   ws_data->wsi = wsi;
1784   ws_data->context = lws_get_context(wsi); // Get context from wsi (not owned)
1785   ws_data->owns_context = false;           // Server owns context, not transport
1786   ws_data->is_connected = true;            // Already connected (server-side)
1787   log_info("[WEBSOCKET_TRANSPORT_CREATE] ★★★ SERVER TRANSPORT CREATED: is_connected=true, wsi=%p, ws_data=%p",
1788            (void *)wsi, (void *)ws_data);
1789   log_debug("Server transport created: is_connected=true, wsi=%p", (void *)wsi);
1790 
1791   // Initialize transport
1792   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1793   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1794   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1795 
1796   log_info("Created WebSocket server transport (crypto: %s)", crypto_ctx ? "enabled" : "disabled");
1797 
1798   // Register transport
1799   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1800 
1801   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1802   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1803 
1804   return transport;
1805 }






References mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(),
    ringbuffer_create(), ringbuffer_destroy(),
    WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, and WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE.








Author


Generated automatically by Doxygen for ascii-chat from the source
    code.







  

    Version 0.8.38
    ascii-chat
